efficiency.note: HTTP-only fetch path with no browser launch overhead.

stability.note: Well-validated for static pages and article extraction.

adaptability.note: Limited to simple pages that do not require JS rendering or challenge handling.

efficiency.note: Rust+V8 headless browser. ~8MB RSS idle, ~50MB peak, 85ms page load. 2-3.5x faster than playwright for JS-rendered pages.

stability.note: v0.1.0 with limited release history. CDP implementation is a subset. Needs more site coverage validation.

adaptability.note: Handles static HTML, JS-rendered SPA, dynamic lists, basic stealth. Not suitable for high-protection pages or complex browser APIs (WebSocket, Web Worker).

efficiency.note: Browser startup adds cost but remains acceptable for default JS-rendered paths.

stability.note: Stable for SPA rendering and dynamic content with existing smoke checks.

adaptability.note: Covers a broad middle tier of dynamic pages without stealth requirements.

efficiency.note: Amortizes browser setup across multiple URLs but remains heavier than single-page get.

stability.note: Expected to be stable for repeated dynamic fetches once smoke-checked.

adaptability.note: Extends batch coverage for the same scenarios as fetch without stealth.

efficiency.note: Heavier browser path because stealth and challenge solving increase runtime cost.

stability.note: Reliable for validated protected-page scenarios once escalation is justified.

adaptability.note: Handles high-protection targets and challenge flows beyond standard fetch.

efficiency.note: Managed browser diagnostics are slower and more operator-intensive than Scrapling.

stability.note: Stable for evidence collection but not the default extraction engine.

adaptability.note: Covers DOM, network, console, and screenshot diagnostics across many edge cases.

efficiency.note: Depends on a live browser tab and is the heaviest operational path.

stability.note: Useful when a real session exists, but continuity depends on the active tab state.

adaptability.note: Best for authenticated continuity and real-session takeovers that other engines cannot preserve.
